children.heading: Definition of qualified hydroelectric facility
children.label: a Definition of qualified hydroelectric facility
A
children.text: is licensed by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission; or

children.label: B
children.text: is a hydroelectric project constructed, operated, or maintained pursuant to a permit or valid existing right-of-way granted prior to , or a license granted pursuant to the Federal Power Act ( et seq.);June 10, 192016 U.S.C. 791a
is placed into service before ; andNovember 15, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: is in compliance with all applicable Federal, Tribal, and State requirements; or

children.label: B
children.text: would be brought into compliance with the requirements described in subparagraph (A) as a result of the capital improvements carried out using an incentive payment under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: adapting more quickly to changing grid conditions;

children.label: B
children.text: providing ancillary services (including black start capabilities, voltage support, and spinning reserves);

children.label: C
children.text: integrating other variable sources of electricity generation; and

children.label: A
children.text: the maintenance or upgrade of spillways or other appurtenant structures;

children.label: B
children.text: dam stability improvements, including erosion repair and enhanced seepage controls; and

children.label: C
children.text: upgrades or replacements of floodgates or natural infrastructure restoration or protection to improve flood risk reduction; or

children.label: A
children.text: adding or improving safe and effective fish passage, including new or upgraded turbine technology, fish ladders, fishways, and all other associated technology, equipment, or other fish passage technology to a qualified hydroelectric facility;

children.label: B
children.text: improving the quality of the water retained or released by a qualified hydroelectric facility;

children.label: C
children.text: promoting downstream sediment transport processes and habitat maintenance; and

children.label: D
children.text: improving recreational access to the project vicinity, including roads, trails, boat ingress and egress, flows to improve recreation, and infrastructure that improves river recreation opportunity.

children.heading: Costs
children.label: 1 Costs
children.text: Incentive payments under this section shall not exceed 30 percent of the costs of the applicable capital improvement.

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: 2 Maximum amount
children.text: Not more than 1 incentive payment may be made under this section with respect to capital improvements at a single qualified hydroelectric facility in any 1 fiscal year, the amount of which shall not exceed $5,000,000.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $553,600,000 for fiscal year 2022, to remain available until expended.
